name: vswobbly
documentation:
# vs-wobbly

A collection of VapourSynth functions for parsing and filtering wobbly files. Full information on how every function works, as well as a list of dependencies and links, can be found in the docstrings of each function and class. For further support, drop by `#dev` in the [JET Discord server](https://discord.gg/XTpc6Fa9eB). ## How to install Install `vswobbly` with the following command: ```shell pip install vswobbly ``` ## How to use Simplest way to use it is to pass a wobbly file (`.wob`) to `WobblyProcessor.from_file()`, followed by calling `apply()`. ```python from vswobbly import WobblyProcessor, wob = WobblyProcessor.from_file('C:/path/to/wobbly.wob') clip = wob.apply() ``` If you only need the parsed wobbly data, you can use `WobblyParser.from_file()`: ```python from vswobbly import WobblyParser wob = WobblyParser.from_file('C:/path/to/wobbly.wob') ``` This will return a `WobblyParser` data class, containing all the relevant data for video processing. Note that metadata, information about wobbly's UI, and wibbly parameters are currently excluded. ### Strategies Different "strategies" can be passed to `WobblyProcessor` to change how certain problems are handled internally. This package comes with a handful included. For example, automatically handling combed frames with vinverse: ```python from vswobbly import WobblyProcessor, DecombVinverseStrategy wob = WobblyProcessor.from_file( 'C:/path/to/wobbly.wob', strategies=[DecombVinverseStrategy()] ) clip = wob.apply() ``` Which would then run the [DecombVinverseStrategy](./vswobbly/process/strategies/combed.py) strategy on all combed frames. This is written to be really flexible, and allow users to handle these problems however they see fit. To implement your own strategy, create a class and inherit from [AbstractProcessingStrategy](./vswobbly/process/strategies/abstract.py). Refer to the existing strategies and the docstrings of the abstract class for examples. Note: For orphan field handling to be handled correctly, the strategy *must* have 'Orphan' in its name.

The clip to process.

The parsed wobbly file. See the  class for more information,

Clip with the processing applied to the selected frames.
```

To write your own strategy, inherit from this class and implement

methods.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.ifades._FrameRangeGrouper.group_frames_into_ranges
documentation:
Group frame numbers into ranges if they are close enough together.

```
param clip

Clip used to determine valid frame range

param frame_nums

List of frame numbers to group

param max_gap

Maximum gap between frames to consider them part of the same range

return

List of (start, end) frame ranges
```
Helper class for grouping frame numbers into ranges.

classes.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.ifades.AdaptiveFixInterlacedFadesStrategy

methods.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.ifades.AdaptiveFixInterlacedFadesStrategy.apply
documentation:
Use FixInterlacedFades to fix interlaced fades, and apply it adaptively.

This works by first combining all (nearly) adjacent frames into a list, and then comparing surrounding frames
to determine if it's a fade from black or white. If it's unsure, it'll average instead.

```
param clip

The clip to process.

param wobbly_parsed

The parsed wobbly file. See the  class for more information,
including all the data that is available.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments.

return

Clip with the processing applied to the selected frames.
```

methods.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.ifades.AdaptiveFixInterlacedFadesStrategy.position
documentation: When to perform filtering.
Strategy for fixing interlaced fades adaptively.

Note that vinverse may be destructive. Be cautious when applying this strategy.

The clip to process.

param wobbly_parsed

including all the data that is available.

Additional keyword arguments.

return

Clip with the processing applied to the selected frames.
```

classes.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.orphans.MatchBasedOrphanQTGMCStrategy

methods.name: vswobbly.process.strategies.orphans.MatchBasedOrphanQTGMCStrategy.apply
documentation:
Apply match-based deinterlacing to the given frames using QTGMC.

This works by using the field match applied to orphan fields
to determine which field is the correct one to keep.
The other field gets deinterlaced.

```
param clip

The clip to process.

param wobbly_parsed

The parsed wobbly file. See the  class for more information,
including all the data that is available.

return

Clip with the processing applied to the selected frames.
```
This is largely copied from my old parser.
This should ideally be rewritten at some point to not use QTGMC.

variables.name: vswobbly.components.types.ValidMatchT
documentation:
Type alias for valid match characters.

p: Match to previous field
c: Match to current field
n: Match to next field
b: Match to previous field (matches from opposite parity field)
u: Match to next field (matches from opposite parity field)

See `http://avisynth.nl/index.php/TIVTC/TFM` for more information.

classes.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFade

methods.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFade.apply
documentation:
Apply the interlaced fade to the frame of the given clip using the specified filter.

If no filter is provided, it will use `vsdeinterlace.fix_interlaced_fades`.

```
param clip

The clip to process.

param filter

The filter to use. The callable must only accept a clip as an argument.

return

The clip with the interlaced fade applied.
```

class_variables.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFade.frame
documentation: The frame number.

class_variables.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFade.field_difference
documentation: The field difference.
Class for holding an interlaced fade.

classes.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFades

methods.name: vswobbly.components.ifades.InterlacedFades.apply
documentation:
Apply the interlaced fades to the clip using the specified filter.

This filter is applied on all frames, so to properly handle dark/bright
and other similar fades, you must handle them in the given filter.

If no filter is provided, it will use `vsdeinterlace.fix_interlaced_fades`.

```
param clip

The clip to process.

param filter

The filter to use. The callable must only accept a clip as an argument.

return

The clip with the interlaced fade applied.
```

methods.name: vswobbly.components.sections.Sections.to_keyframes
documentation:
Convert the sections to keyframes.

Accounts for decimated frames by adjusting section start frames
based on the number of decimations that occur before each section start.

```
param decimations

The decimations to account for.

return

A keyframes object representing the section start frames
adjusted for decimations.
```
